column1: # Custom partition table for this board's 8 MB flash — based on Arduino-ESP32's
# stock default_8MB.csv
column2: with one change: the unused stock spiffs partition

column1: # (this project never used a filesystem) is replaced by a smaller "cfgdata"
# partition (64 KB
# namespace (see config.cpp) — WiFi/MQTT settings
# data
# Deliberately a SEPARATE partition rather than simply growing the stock
# "nvs" partition: "nvs" is also used by WiFiManager/ESP-IDF's own WiFi
# credential storage
column2: and — on this same physical board — by at least one

column1: # other
column2: unrelated firmware project that happens to share our "config"

column1: # namespace name. Keeping "nvs" at its stock offset/size means:
#   - flashing this firmware over EITHER an older build of this project OR
#     that other project leaves its "nvs" partition (and whatever it stored
#     there) completely undisturbed — no page-boundary ambiguity
#     namespace collision with our own data anymore.
#   - flashing an OLDER firmware (stock partition table) back over THIS one
#     is equally safe for the same reason: "nvs" never changed shape
#     nothing that only knows about "nvs" is affected either way. It simply
#     won't know "cfgdata" exists
# cfgdata is placed AFTER app0/app1 (where spiffs used to be)
# so app0/app1 keep their EXACT stock offsets (0x10000 / 0x340000). This
# matters because PlatformIO's esptool upload step does not derive the
# firmware's flash offset from this file's app0 entry; it always defaults
# to board.upload.offset_address = 0x10000 unless told otherwise (a real
# footgun found the hard way: an earlier version of this table moved app0
# to make room for cfgdata *before* it
# firmware to the old 0x10000 — right into the middle of cfgdata — until
# board_upload.offset_address was added to platformio.ini to match). With
# app0 back at the stock offset
column2: no such override is needed at all

column1: # less thing can drift out of sync between this file and platformio.ini.
# It also means this table is now byte-identical to the stock one for nvs
# otadata
column3: AND app1 — the only difference is what used to be spiffs.

column1: # That also directly benefits OTA updates via WiFiManager's own /update page
# (or ArduinoOTA): OTA never rewrites the partition table itself
# app image in whichever OTA slot the table currently defines — so flashing
# this project and that other (stock-partition-table) one back and forth
# via OTA is safe in both directions
column2: regardless of which one is currently

column1: # "in flash": "nvs" (used by both projects' actual WiFi state
# other project's "config" namespace) is never touched
# simply ignored by firmware that doesn't know about it.
# A full flash erase before the next upload is still recommended whenever
# this table changes
# Name
nvs
otadata
app0
app1
cfgdata
coredump
